QF.id: XXVA Sales and Distribution of Research Services_3

question.text: A research report typically contains two types of sections — fact-based and view-based. Which of the following is a view-based section?

answer.text: Peer group analysis with financial comparisons
Key strengths and concerns about the company based on management interaction and personal understanding
Key financial indicators and ratio analysis

explanation.text: **The Logic:** View-based sections include Company Business overview, Key Strengths, Key Concerns, and Industry Overview — these require management interaction and personal/professional understanding. Peer group analysis, shareholding pattern, and financial indicators are fact-based. **The Trap:** Fact-based sections draw from annual reports, quarterly results, and calculations. View-based sections draw from qualitative assessment and professional judgment. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series XV Chapter 13 distinguishes fact-based and view-based sections. Series X-B covers similar analysis frameworks. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The value-add of research is in the view-based sections — any algorithm can compile financial data, but insightful qualitative analysis requires human judgment. Highlight these sections when presenting research to clients.
